title: [WIP] machine: search for `qemu-kvm` binary on linux
body:
If the `qemu-system-{x86_64, aarch64}` binary is unavailable on linux, search for `qemu-kvm` as well.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

For more detailed information, please review our contributing guidelines:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests
-->

#### Checklist

Ensure you have completed the following checklist for your pull request to be reviewed:
<!-- Use [x] to mark as done, or click the checkbox after opening PR -->

- [x] Certify you wrote the patch or otherwise have the right to pass it on as an open-source patch by signing all
commits. (`git commit -s`). (If needed, use `git commit -s --amend`).  The author email must match
the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [x] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)
- [ ] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [ ] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [ ] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [ ] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
Write `None` if there are no user-facing changes, otherwise enter your release note below.
Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->

```release-note
Fixes a bug on Linux where Podman doesn't search for `qemu-kvm` if `qemu-system-{x86_64, aarch64}` isn't installed
```

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *jakecorrenti* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign [luap99](https://github.com/luap99) for approval. For more information see [the Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Ok I did a quick check on debian and they don't add `qemu-kvm` but they use `kvm` which is just a symlink to qemu-system-x86_64 so I guess it makes the most sense to stay with qemu-system-x86_64 as default.

Tbh I wonder if this should be even in the upstream code if RHEL is the only one who needs this special dance...

Since I don't want to add more dirs in https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/538 we need a symlink in the podman-machine rpm no matter what. And if we do that we might as well link `/usr/libexec/podman/qemu-system-x86_64` to `/usr/libexec/qemu-kvm` and do not need any podman code changes at all. Just a minor rpm addition.

> Since I don't want to add more dirs in [containers/container-libs#538](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/538) we need a symlink in the podman-machine rpm no matter what. And if we do that we might as well link `/usr/libexec/podman/qemu-system-x86_64 to `/usr/libexec/qemu-kvm` and do not need any podman code changes at all. Just a minor rpm addition.

Just to make sure I'm understanding everything, this is purely an RPM issue and should not impact the containers/container-libs or podman upstream repos? 

cc/ @lsm5

> > Since I don't want to add more dirs in [containers/container-libs#538](https://github.com/containers/container-libs/pull/538) we need a symlink in the podman-machine rpm no matter what. And if we do that we might as well link `/usr/libexec/podman/qemu-system-x86_64 to `/usr/libexec/qemu-kvm` and do not need any podman code changes at all. Just a minor rpm addition.
> 
> Just to make sure I'm understanding everything, this is purely an RPM issue and should not impact the containers/container-libs or podman upstream repos?
> 
> cc/ @lsm5

Yeah we would only have to patch that in the RHEL specfile so @jnovy would be the person for that. The fedora specfile would not need these links. I am not sure what the status is and if the specfiles are synced from upstream to RHEL these days or not. If we have it synced we need to make the change here
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/f66f7c8a5b142f2452a61eced63e16d0ca30b4e7/rpm/podman.spec#L323-L326

> Yeah we would only have to patch that in the RHEL specfile so @jnovy would be the person for that. The fedora specfile would not need these links. I am not sure what the status is and if the specfiles are synced from upstream to RHEL these days or not. If we have it synced we need to make the change here

There's no syncing of spec files from upstream to CentOS Stream/RHEL currently. @jnovy gets those changes in independently.

While Fedora doesn't need this, I think we should still include the change as a RHEL conditional in upstream `rpm/podman.spec` for any ELN testing and also to not be caught by surprise whenever the next CentOS Stream / RHEL branches out from Fedora sources.
I can take this PR over if we're sure to proceed with just the packaging changes.
Please write to me which symlink to where do you actually need to create @Luap99 @lsm5 in RHEL.

Untested, but I think this should work there assuming `%{_arch}` is the proper macro to use and resolves to `x86_64` and 
`aarch64` respectively.
```
ln -s ../qemu-kvm %{buildroot}%{_libexecdir}/%{name}/qemu-system-%{_arch}
```
@jnovy @lsm5 just following up to confirm who's taking this over, if anyone

> @jnovy @lsm5 just following up to confirm who's taking this over, if anyone

@jakecorrenti I'll create a new PR for upstream, @jnovy will handle RHEL. Feel free to close this if there's nothing else to do here.
@lsm5 Just created https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27773 - sorry, I read your message too late. Once merged it will be propadated to CentOS.
